title: Drynaria angustiloba
intro: Drynaria angustiloba, also known as the narrow-leaved polydichotomum, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-boosting properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Drynaria angustiloba include various triterpenoids, including polysaccharides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, capsules, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Drynaria angustiloba is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae and the genus Drynaria. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the division Pteridophyta. As a member of the Polypodiaceae family, it is closely related to other fern species such as Drynaria quercifolia. The genus Drynaria is characterized by its terrestrial, rhizomatous growth habit. Drynaria angustiloba is native to Southeast Asia and is commonly found in tropical forests and shaded areas.
